design_helpers.save_disabled_google_oauth: Save is unlocked after Google consent returns a refresh token.
design_helpers.save_disabled_provider_oauth_with_hc: Save is unlocked after {label} authorization and connection test succeed.

design_helpers.oauth_hint_google: One click consent using app-managed Google OAuth. You can uncheck permissions on the consent screen.
design_helpers.oauth_hint_provider: Enter your {label} OAuth client credentials above, then click to authorize.
design_helpers.hc_timeout_friendly: Could not reach the service -- the request timed out.
design_helpers.hc_timeout_suggestion: Check that the URL is correct and the service is online. Your firewall or proxy may be blocking the connection.
design_helpers.hc_dns_friendly: Could not reach the service -- the hostname could not be resolved.
design_helpers.hc_dns_suggestion: Double-check the service URL for typos. Make sure you have an active internet connection.
design_helpers.hc_refused_suggestion: The service may be down or the URL/port may be incorrect. Verify the endpoint address.
design_helpers.hc_unreachable_friendly: Could not reach the service -- check the URL.
design_helpers.hc_unreachable_suggestion: Verify your internet connection and that the service endpoint is correct.
design_helpers.hc_401_friendly: Your credentials appear to be invalid or expired.
design_helpers.hc_401_suggestion: Double-check the API key or token -- make sure you copied the full value without extra spaces.
design_helpers.hc_403_friendly: Your credentials lack the required permissions.
design_helpers.hc_403_suggestion: The key is valid but does not have access to this endpoint. Check your API key's scopes or role assignments.
design_helpers.hc_404_friendly: The healthcheck endpoint was not found.
design_helpers.hc_404_suggestion: This usually means the generated test URL is wrong -- not a problem with your credentials. Try saving anyway if you trust the key.
design_helpers.hc_429_friendly: Too many requests -- the service is rate-limiting you.
design_helpers.hc_429_suggestion: Wait a moment and try again. This doesn't necessarily mean your credentials are wrong.
design_helpers.hc_5xx_friendly: The service returned a server error.
design_helpers.hc_5xx_suggestion: This is likely a temporary issue on the provider's side. Try again in a few minutes.
design_helpers.hc_4xx_suggestion: Verify your credentials and ensure the API key has the correct permissions.
design_helpers.hc_skipped_friendly: Automatic testing was skipped for this service.
design_helpers.hc_skipped_suggestion: You can save the credential and test it manually by running your agent.
design_helpers.tip_too_short: Your description was quite short. Try being more specific -- e.g. "GitHub personal access token" instead of "GitHub".
design_helpers.tip_missing_credential_type: Include the credential type -- e.g. "API key", "OAuth", "bot token", or "secret key".
design_helpers.tip_specific_service: Mention the specific service or product name clearly (e.g. "Stripe" rather than "payment processor").
design_helpers.tip_simpler_description: Try a simpler, more targeted description to speed up analysis.
design_helpers.tip_internet_check: Check your internet connection -- the AI needs to reach Anthropic servers.
design_helpers.tip_restart_app: After installing, restart the app and try again.
design_helpers.tip_rephrase: Try rephrasing your request with the service name and credential type.
design_helpers.tip_simpler_first: If the issue persists, try a simpler description first, then add details.

Patterns are matched as substrings against the combined title and content of each submission. When a pattern matches, the configured action is applied. consent.title: Welcome to Personas Desktop
consent.subtitle: Please review how this application works before continuing
consent.intro: Personas Desktop is a local-first AI agent orchestration tool. Before you start, please understand what it does and what data it accesses.
consent.ai_tldr: Your prompts are sent to your chosen AI service using your own API key.
consent.ai_detail_1: Your persona prompts, tool definitions, and input data are sent to the selected AI provider (Anthropic Claude, OpenAI Codex, Google Gemini, or GitHub Copilot) for execution.
consent.ai_detail_2: Requests are made over HTTPS to provider APIs. Each provider has its own terms of service and data retention policies.
consent.ai_detail_3: You supply your own API keys; they are encrypted locally and never shared with us.
consent.ai_detail_4: Execution output (including token counts and cost) is stored locally in your database.
consent.storage_tldr: Your passwords are encrypted and all your data stays on your computer.
consent.storage_detail_1: All data (personas, execution history, logs, memories) is stored in a local SQLite database on your machine.
consent.storage_detail_2: Credential values (API keys, tokens, passwords) are encrypted with AES-256-GCM before storage. The encryption key is held in your OS keyring.
consent.storage_detail_3: Execution logs are written as plaintext files to your app data directory.
consent.storage_detail_4: No data is sent to Personas servers unless you explicitly use the optional cloud deployment feature.
consent.services_tldr: Agents can connect to services like Slack or GitHub using credentials you provide.
consent.services_detail_1: Personas can make authenticated API calls to 40+ services (Slack, GitHub, Linear, Discord, Jira, Notion, databases, etc.) using credentials you provide.
consent.services_detail_2: These calls are made on your behalf using your credentials. You control which services each persona can access.
consent.services_detail_3: An API proxy validates URLs against a blocklist (private IPs, localhost) to mitigate SSRF risks.
consent.monitoring_tldr: Agents can watch your clipboard, files, or schedule to trigger actions automatically.
consent.monitoring_clipboard: Clipboard Monitor: When enabled for a persona, the app polls your system clipboard (~500ms interval) to detect text changes matching configured regex patterns. Clipboard content is hashed for change detection and is not stored unless it triggers an execution.
consent.monitoring_file: File Watcher: When configured, monitors specified local directories for file creation or modification to trigger persona execution.
consent.monitoring_cron: Cron Scheduler: Runs personas on configured schedules (cron expressions). Active only while the app is running.
consent.monitoring_webhook: Webhook Server: A local HTTP server (localhost:9420) listens for inbound webhooks when webhook triggers are configured.
consent.p2p_tldr: The app can discover and communicate with other Personas instances on your local network.
consent.p2p_detail_1: When enabled, the app broadcasts its presence on your local network via mDNS (_personas._tcp.local.) so other Personas Desktop instances can discover it.
consent.p2p_detail_2: A QUIC transport binds on all network interfaces (0.0.0.0) to accept connections from LAN peers. Only peers with trusted cryptographic identities can exchange data.
consent.p2p_detail_3: Connected peers can exchange persona manifests (names, descriptions, tool lists) and agent-to-agent messages. Credential values are never shared over P2P.
consent.p2p_detail_4: Share links serve persona bundles over HTTP on your local network. These links are only accessible to devices on the same network.
consent.foraging_tldr: The app can scan your filesystem to discover existing credentials and read third-party app data.
consent.foraging_detail_1: The Credential Discovery feature scans known paths on your filesystem (e.g., ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.ssh/) to detect existing API keys and tokens you may want to import.
consent.foraging_detail_2: Discovered credential files are read only when you explicitly trigger a scan. Found credentials are shown for review before any import.
consent.foraging_detail_3: Obsidian Brain integration reads Obsidian vault configuration and note files from known OS paths when you enable the feature.
consent.foraging_detail_4: No filesystem data is transmitted externally. All scanned data remains local unless you explicitly import it into the app.
consent.process_tldr: The app runs AI tools and scripts on your machine to carry out agent tasks.
consent.process_detail_1: The app spawns AI provider CLI processes (e.g., claude, codex, gemini) as child processes on your machine.
consent.process_detail_2: Credentials are passed to child processes as environment variables (not CLI arguments) and scrubbed after execution.
consent.process_detail_3: Browser automation (Auto-Credential setup) may launch a Playwright-controlled browser session to help set up OAuth credentials. This requires your explicit consent each time.
consent.process_detail_4: Automations can trigger external workflows (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI/CD, n8n, webhooks) based on execution output.
consent.telemetry_tldr: Anonymous crash reports and usage data help fix bugs -- you can opt out at any time.
consent.telemetry_detail_1: Crash reports may be sent to Sentry for error tracking. IP addresses, email addresses, and request bodies are stripped before transmission.
consent.telemetry_detail_2: Anonymous feature usage data (which sections and tabs you visit) is sent to Sentry to help prioritize development. Anonymous device IDs are used for session counting.
consent.telemetry_detail_3: No personal data, credential values, or execution content is included in any telemetry.
consent.telemetry_detail_4: You can disable all telemetry at any time in Settings > Account. The app checks for updates via GitHub Releases.
consent.deploy_tldr: You can optionally run agents in the cloud -- nothing is uploaded unless you choose to.

obsidian_brain.step1_desc: Grant Personas access to create files in your Drive. Only the app's own folder is accessible.
obsidian_brain.step2_desc: Vault notes are uploaded as markdown files to Drive. Only changed files are synced (content-hash comparison).
obsidian_brain.step3_desc: Pull on another device to download. Your 15 GB free Google Drive storage is more than enough for thousands of notes.
obsidian_brain.vault_connection_subtitle: Connect to an Obsidian vault for bidirectional sync
obsidian_brain.mirror_research_lab_desc: Save Research Lab experiments to your vault as notes.
obsidian_brain.mirror_execution_knowledge_desc: Write what agents learn from executions into your vault.
obsidian_brain.mirror_athena_desc: Let Athena read your vault for specific cases and analysis.
obsidian_brain.memories_desc: Persona memories with category, importance, and tags
obsidian_brain.persona_profiles_desc: System prompts, config, and design context
obsidian_brain.auto_sync_desc: Automatically push changes when memories are created
obsidian_brain.folder_structure_subtitle: Customize how data is organized in your vault
obsidian_brain.diff_truncated: Diff truncated — open the note to see the full content.
obsidian_brain.no_sync_activity: No sync activity yet. Push or pull to start.
obsidian_brain.select_note_hint: Choose a note from the tree on the left to preview its contents.
obsidian_brain.saved_vaults_empty_hint: Connect and save a vault to see it here.
obsidian_brain.saved_vaults_empty_hint_setup: Save a vault configuration to see it here. You can switch between vaults anytime.
obsidian_brain.saved_vaults_empty_hint_other: No saved vaults yet. Set one up in the Setup tab.
obsidian_brain.vault_stats_subtitle: Graph metrics across all notes in the active vault
obsidian_brain.search_vault_subtitle: Substring search over note titles and bodies
obsidian_brain.orphan_notes_empty: No orphan notes — every note is linked from somewhere.
obsidian_brain.mocs_empty: No MOCs detected — try lowering the link threshold.
obsidian_brain.quick_journal_subtitle: Append a section to today's daily note
obsidian_brain.journal_body_placeholder: What happened? What did you learn? Drop it here and it ends up in today's daily note.
obsidian_brain.capture_meeting_subtitle: Write a structured meeting note under Meetings/
obsidian_brain.no_vaults_detected: No Obsidian vaults detected. Try browsing manually.
obsidian_brain.all_vaults_saved: All detected vaults are already saved. Add a new one via Browse.
obsidian_brain.select_and_test_first: Please select and test a valid vault first

companion.name: Athena
companion.tracking_subtitle: Athena absorbs CLI activity (git commits + active-runs ledger) into hourly per-project pulses she'll discuss with you when Dev Tools is on. Per-project flags live in Dev Tools → Lifecycle → Project Tracking.
companion.tracking_master_on: ON — Sonnet consolidator runs every hour for enabled projects.
companion.tracking_master_off: OFF — no ingestion, no LLM, no brain writes.
companion.footer_hold_to_talk: Tap to open · hold to talk
companion.orb_talk_hint: Tap to open · hold to talk · drag to move
companion.guide_click_hint: Click the highlighted area to continue
companion.decision_composing: Composing the full picture in your Cockpit…
companion.decision_compose_failed: Couldn't compose the visual explanation — the recommendation above still stands.
companion.decision_recommend_approve: This looks low-risk — I'd approve it.
companion.decision_recommend_review: I'd take a closer look before approving this one.
companion.decision_recommend_resolve: This is blocking work — I'd resolve it now.
companion.decision_recommend_review_open: Open the review to see the full context before deciding.
companion.guide_pc_intro: Let me walk you through creating a persona — watch where I point.
companion.guide_pc_open: This is the build studio, where every persona starts.
companion.guide_pc_compose: This is where you begin — tap the center and describe, in plain language, what you want your persona to do.
companion.guide_pc_autonomous: Prefer hands-off? Flip this on and I'll design, build, and test the whole persona myself.
companion.guide_pc_outro: That's the whole flow. Want me to build one for you now?
companion.guide_conn_intro: Want to connect a service like GitHub or Slack? Let me show you where.
companion.guide_conn_vault: This is your Vault — every connection lives here, with its secrets encrypted on your device.
companion.guide_conn_add: Open "Add new" and my setup wizard walks you through any connector: paste a key or sign in, and it's ready to use.
companion.guide_conn_outro: That's it. Once a credential is here, pin it to a persona and it can use the service.
companion.offer_intro: I can help you create that persona.
companion.offer_build_hint: I'll generate, test, and fix it automatically.
companion.offer_show: Show me how to build it
companion.offer_show_hint: I'll walk you through the studio step by step.
companion.stt_desc: Choose how Athena hears you when you hold to talk.
companion.stt_engine_browser_caption: Built-in, zero setup. Audio is sent to the system speech service.
companion.stt_engine_whisper_caption: On-device transcription. Audio never leaves your machine.
companion.stt_browser_disclosure: On this platform, browser dictation streams your microphone audio to the OS vendor's cloud speech service. Switch to Local Whisper to keep audio on-device.
companion.stt_engine_status_desc: Local transcription needs the whisper.cpp binary installed.
companion.stt_install_hint: Drop the whisper.cpp binary at the path below (or set PERSONAS_WHISPER_BIN). Get a build from github.com/ggerganov/whisper.cpp/releases.
companion.stt_models_desc: Download a model, then select it. Smaller = faster; larger = more accurate.
companion.hello_phase_zero: I'm here, but I haven't found my voice yet — chat lands in the next phase.
companion.welcome_subtitle: I can see your agents, executions, and memory. Ask me anything, or start with one of these.
companion.slow_progress_soft: Still working — taking a bit longer than usual.
companion.slow_progress_firm: Athena is still working. Click the stop button if you'd like to interrupt.
companion.recall_empty: Athena spoke from a blank brain this turn.
companion.recall_combined: Athena replayed {episodes} recent turns and consulted {memories} memories.
companion.recall_synthesized_tooltip: Raw recall was over budget — Athena summarized it into a focused briefing before responding.
companion.connector_call_in_flight_hint: Athena will summarize the result when this finishes.
companion.autonomous_toggle_on: Enable autonomous mode (Athena keeps working between turns)
companion.composer_placeholder: Talk to Athena. Enter to send, Shift+Enter for a new line.
companion.slash_message_queued: What jobs are queued right now?
companion.slash_message_decisions: Read back the recent design decisions.
companion.slash_message_live_ops: What's running in the fleet right now?
companion.slash_message_memory_recap: What do you remember about me?
companion.doctrine_up_to_date: Athena's docs are up to date.
companion.analyze_fleet_started: Fleet analysis started — Athena is reviewing the teams.
companion.daily_brief_started: Daily brief started — Athena is reviewing your last 24h.
companion.voice_test_unconfigured: Configure a voice in the Voice tab to test.
companion.voice_test_sentence: Hello, I am Athena, your personal AI assistant.
companion.brain_empty_hint: This memory fills in as you and Athena work together.
companion.brain_empty_ask_prompt: Help me build out your {kind} memory — what belongs here, and can we add the first entry now?
companion.brain_empty_consolidation_started: Consolidation started — review the proposals in the Memory tab.
companion.brain_desc_identity: Who Athena understands you to be — role, preferences, working style.
companion.brain_desc_facts_user: Durable facts about you she's learned and reuses across sessions.
companion.brain_desc_facts_project: What she knows about your active projects and their context.
companion.brain_desc_facts_world: General facts not tied to you or a specific project.
companion.brain_desc_goals: What you're working toward, so she can track progress.
companion.brain_desc_backlog: Ideas and tasks parked for later.
companion.brain_desc_procedurals: Repeatable how-to steps she's agreed to follow.
companion.brain_desc_rituals: Recurring routines she runs on a cadence.
companion.brain_desc_episodes: A log of past conversations she can replay.
companion.brain_desc_reflections: Periodic self-summaries of what she's learned.
companion.brain_desc_design_decisions: Choices made while designing personas, with rationale.
companion.brain_desc_doctrine: Reference guides that shape how she reasons.
companion.brain_desc_constitution: Her core operating contract — the rules she won't break.
companion.facts_subtitle: Distilled facts with source episodes — the anti-hallucination contract.
companion.facts_empty_user: No facts about you yet. Try asking Athena to remember something specific.
companion.setup_memory_desc: Tune how Athena recalls and shapes context for each turn.
companion.setup_recall_synthesis_desc: On dense-recall turns, fold the brain's retrieved chunks through a Claude call into a focused briefing. Adds ~1 extra Claude call on qualifying turns; below the budget threshold no synthesis fires.
companion.setup_hands_free_decisions_desc: Surface pending approvals, reviews, and blocking incidents as numbered choices above the orb, one at a time.
companion.setup_desktop_desc: What Athena observes from your desktop. All sources start off. Disabling a source also clears what was already captured.
companion.setup_desktop_clipboard_desc: Track when the clipboard changes (metadata only — content not stored).
companion.setup_desktop_file_changes_desc: Watch declared work-roots for create / modify / delete events.
companion.setup_desktop_app_focus_desc: Track which app is in the foreground. Window titles redacted at capture (paths reduced to filenames, emails masked).
companion.setup_desktop_cli_session_desc: Let opted-in personas read your most recent Claude CLI conversation as prompt context. Per-persona opt-in required; only the last 8 turns are extracted, capped to 500 chars each.
companion.sensory_cli_reads_empty_hint: When a persona with CLI awareness opted in fires while you're mid-conversation in `claude`, the read will be logged here.
companion.setup_desktop_load_failed: Couldn't load desktop-awareness state — check the backend connection.
companion.sensory_signals_subtitle: Recent signals captured by the desktop-awareness watchers. Delete any row to remove it from Athena's rolling window.
companion.sensory_signals_empty_hint: Either the watchers are off (Setup → Desktop awareness) or the rolling window is empty.
companion.footer_notice_proactive_default: Athena has a note for you
companion.chat_card_unknown_kind: Athena tried to show a card we don't recognize yet — looks like something updated. Reopen the chat to refresh.
companion.persona_overview_hover_hint: Hover a node to inspect it. Click to open in the editor.
companion.persona_overview_next_step_pick_body: Tap a persona to open it in the editor.
companion.persona_overview_next_step_tune_body: Adjust model tier, budget, or trust score.
companion.persona_overview_next_step_run_body: Send a real input from the play surface.
companion.walkthrough_empty: Athena's walkthrough card was empty — try asking again with more specifics.
companion.browser_report_empty: The browser-test report card was empty.
companion.browser_bridge_desc: Pair the Athena Browser Bridge extension so live tests run in your own Chrome. Without it, tests fall back to a bundled browser.
companion.browser_bridge_env_override: The token is set by the PERSONAS_BROWSER_BRIDGE_TOKEN environment variable for this run — the stored token is inactive.
companion.browser_bridge_hint: Load the extension from tools/athena-browser-extension (chrome://extensions → Load unpacked), then paste the port and token into its options page.
companion.template_suggestions_empty: No templates in your gallery match this intent yet. Worth a build from scratch — or browse the full catalog.
companion.use_case_set_empty: Athena's use-case proposal was empty — try asking again.
companion.trigger_set_empty: Athena's trigger proposal was empty — try asking again.
companion.model_tier_empty: Athena's tier comparison was empty — try asking again.
companion.observability_plan_empty: Athena's observability plan was empty — try asking again.
companion.decision_log_empty: Athena hasn't logged any decisions for this conversation yet.
companion.decision_log_persisted_tooltip: Athena writes every decision_log entry into companion_design_decision so the audit trail survives session reloads.
companion.persona_ready_empty: Athena's recap needs at least a refined intent line. Try asking again.
companion.persona_ready_hint_interactive: Build with the standard ask-the-user gate flow.
companion.persona_ready_hint_oneshot: Athena will decide everything and ping you when done.
companion.persona_ready_hint_template: Open the template gallery to pick a starter.
companion.design_cap_title: What I can help you design
companion.design_cap_walkthrough_behavior: I sketch the full plan: intent line, prompt outline, use cases, tools, triggers, tier, observability.
companion.design_cap_walkthrough_example: "Help me design a persona that triages support tickets."
companion.design_cap_templates_behavior: I match your intent against the template gallery and surface the top 3 starters.
companion.design_cap_use_cases_behavior: I propose 3-5 use cases tagged golden / variant / out-of-scope so coverage is glanceable.
companion.design_cap_use_cases_example: "What use cases would this persona need?"
companion.design_cap_triggers_behavior: I propose 1-4 trigger configurations with source, condition, grain, idempotency notes.
companion.design_cap_tier_behavior: I compare Haiku / Sonnet / Opus for this intent and recommend one with rationale.
companion.design_cap_observability_behavior: I draft the error-handling and success-metric pair so this persona never black-holes.
companion.design_cap_decision_log_behavior: I track design choices during the conversation as a timeline so you can retrace later.
companion.design_cap_decision_log_example: "Show me what we've decided so far."
companion.design_cap_ready_behavior: I roll everything into one build-ready card with a commit button when the design is locked in.
companion.memory_bulk_actions_desc: Manual maintenance passes that distill conversations into long-lived facts and surface patterns.
companion.consolidation_running_long: Athena is reviewing your recent conversations and proposing fact updates. This usually takes 1-3 minutes.
companion.consolidation_review_subtitle: Each row is a proposed fact. Approve, reject, or edit before it lands in memory.
companion.consolidation_no_proposals: Athena had nothing new to consolidate from this batch.
companion.decay_none: No facts old enough to decay yet.
companion.improve_send_title: Send as a self-improve request — Athena will run a coding session at the repo and apply the change.
companion.page_subtitle: Setup, memory inspector, and voice configuration for your thinking partner.
companion.decisions_panel_subtitle: Every design choice Athena has logged across conversations. Filter by persona context to scope.
companion.decisions_panel_empty: Athena hasn't logged any design decisions yet. Ask her to design a persona to see this fill up.
companion.decisions_panel_empty_cta: Ask Athena to log a decision
companion.decisions_panel_empty_prompt: I'd like to log a design decision. Ask me what I chose, the alternatives I weighed, and my reasoning — then record it for me.
companion.decisions_panel_empty_filtered: No decisions match that context. Try a shorter filter or clear it.
companion.dashboard_empty_body: Ask Athena to compose one for you — for example, "Show me my agent activity for the past week."
companion.setup_chrome_desc: Where Athena shows up in the app and how she gets your attention.
companion.setup_footer_desc: Display the bot button in the bottom-left footer. When off, the chat panel is unreachable except via this plugin page.
companion.setup_orb_desc: Show Athena as a draggable orb that floats above the app. Tap to open chat, hold to talk, drag to dock anywhere. When off, only the footer button remains.
companion.setup_sound_desc: Play a short two-note tone when Athena finishes a reply, even if you've tabbed away.
companion.setup_beta_desc: Experimental capabilities gated behind backend flags. Status is read-only here — flipping requires a build change.
companion.setup_self_improve_on: Available. The wrench-send button on the composer triggers a separate Claude CLI coding session that edits the repo immediately.
companion.setup_self_improve_off: Disabled in this build. The composer's wrench-send button is hidden.
companion.voice_subtitle: Have Athena speak her replies via ElevenLabs. Configuration only — playback in chat is downstream.
companion.voice_empty_desc: Voice playback runs through your own ElevenLabs API key. Add a credential in the vault to continue.
companion.voice_credential_desc: Which ElevenLabs key Athena uses for synthesis.
companion.voice_id_desc: The ElevenLabs voice id Athena should use. Find it on the ElevenLabs voice library page.
companion.voice_id_hint: 21–32 characters, alphanumeric. Each voice has a unique id you can copy from the ElevenLabs library.
companion.voice_enable_desc: Master switch for voice playback. Requires a credential and a voice id.
companion.voice_enable_on: Athena will speak her replies aloud. Playback wiring lands in a follow-up.
companion.voice_enable_blocked: Pick a credential and enter a voice id first.
companion.voice_pick_desc: Pick from voices scoped to this credential, or list every voice on the account.
companion.voice_pick_no_voices: No voices found on this account.
companion.voice_pick_scoped_hint: Showing voices scoped to this credential. Edit scope from the credential detail to change.
companion.voice_pick_unscoped_hint: No scope set — listing every voice on this account. Add a scope in the credential detail to narrow this down.
companion.voice_settings_desc: Tune model, expressiveness, and pace. Leave on default unless Athena's voice feels off.
companion.voice_settings_model_hint: Turbo is the fastest and cheapest. Multilingual and v3 are the most expressive but slower.
companion.voice_settings_stability_hint: Lower = more emotion and variation. Higher = more consistent and monotone.
companion.voice_settings_similarity_hint: How closely Athena should match the picked voice's reference recordings.
companion.voice_settings_speed_hint: Speech rate. Range 0.7–1.2; default speed sits in the middle. Leave at default to omit.
companion.voice_settings_style_hint: Only meaningful on Multilingual v2 and v3. Higher values amplify expressive cues at the cost of consistency.
companion.voice_engine_desc: Cloud (ElevenLabs) is the default. Piper runs locally on your machine — no network, no API key — but needs a one-time install.
companion.voice_piper_engine_status_desc: Athena synthesizes with the official Piper binary. Install once; voices download separately below.
companion.voice_piper_engine_install_hint_release: Releases at github.com/rhasspy/piper/releases (the Windows zip contains piper.exe + onnxruntime.dll)
companion.voice_piper_voices_desc: Pick a voice and click Download. Models live under ~/.personas/companion-tts/piper/ and are ~7–110 MB depending on quality.
companion.voice_piper_no_voice_selected: Pick a voice from the list below to enable Piper synthesis.
companion.voice_piper_engine_missing_callout: Install the Piper engine first to use the selected voice.
companion.voice_piper_no_voices_for_language: No Piper voice covers your locale yet. Try a different language or fall back to ElevenLabs.
companion.dictate_start_hint: Press to talk. Audio is sent to the browser's cloud speech-to-text service while listening.
companion.dictate_error: Dictation failed. Check microphone permission and try again.

orchestration.guidance_placeholder: Type your answer — claude is paused on this RPC.

companion.wake_cadence_hint: How long signals accumulate before Athena wakes and handles them in one batch. Blocked teams and urgent messages bypass the timer.

ocr.title: OCR
ocr.subtitle: Extract and compare text from images and documents
ocr.extract_desc: Upload an image or PDF and extract text using Gemini Vision API or Claude Code CLI.
ocr.compare_desc: Run both providers on the same file and compare outputs. Gemini uses API key, Claude uses your subscription.
ocr.no_results_hint: Extract text from a document to see it here.

dev_context.scanning_subtitle: Analyzing codebase patterns and generating context groups…

dev_lifecycle.uat_scenario_placeholder: e.g. add a todo, mark it done, clear completed, check the console for errors
dev_lifecycle.uat_url_label: Target URL (optional — defaults to the project's test environment)
dev_lifecycle.uat_blocked_todos: Finish the other to-dos first — the browser test is the final acceptance step.
dev_lifecycle.uat_gate_hint: This goal can't be marked done until the browser UAT passes.
dev_lifecycle.uat_web_only: Browser UAT is available for web projects only.
dev_lifecycle.uat_mark_passed_confirm: Mark the UAT as passed without running it?
dev_lifecycle.timeline_no_dated_sub: Set target dates on your goals to see them on the timeline.
dev_lifecycle.goal_empty_subtitle: Set the high-level direction for this project. Create goals, link teams to advance them, and let Athena track progress.
dev_lifecycle.goal_ask_athena: Ask Athena to set up goals
dev_lifecycle.goal_ask_athena_prompt: Help me set up development goals for this project. Suggest a few high-level goals based on what you know about it, and we can refine them together.
dev_lifecycle.goal_scaffold_timeline_sub: A time-axis view of goals by target date, with dependency arrows and slip markers. Coming soon.
dev_lifecycle.goal_field_title_placeholder: What outcome are you driving toward?
dev_lifecycle.goal_field_description_placeholder: Optional context, scope, or what success looks like…
dev_lifecycle.goal_explainer_board: Each card is a goal. "Your turn" holds goals that need you; "Agent's turn" is what your AI team is working on.
dev_lifecycle.goal_explainer_map: Goals arranged by how they connect — arrows show what needs to happen first. Drag to arrange, click a goal for details.
dev_lifecycle.goal_explainer_timeline: Goals laid out by target date, grouped by week and month. Goals without a date sit in their own group.
dev_lifecycle.goal_starters_label: Or start with a common goal:
dev_lifecycle.goal_starter_release_desc: Get a first version into users' hands — decide what's in, finish it, and publish.
dev_lifecycle.goal_starter_bugs_desc: Collect the issues that hurt users most and work through them one by one.
dev_lifecycle.goal_starter_docs_desc: Explain what the project does and how a new person gets it running.
dev_lifecycle.goal_starter_quality_desc: Add checks and tests around the riskiest parts so changes stop breaking things.
dev_lifecycle.goal_dep_add_depends_on: + Add a goal this needs first…
dev_lifecycle.goal_dep_add_follows: + Add a goal this comes after…
dev_lifecycle.goal_advance_active: A team assignment is already advancing this goal.
dev_lifecycle.goal_advance_hint: Builds an assignment from the open to-dos (or decomposes the goal) and runs it.
dev_lifecycle.goal_handoff_label: Hand off to your AI team
dev_lifecycle.goal_handoff_explain: Your AI team will work through the to-dos here — or break the goal into steps — then start working. You can review what they do as it happens.
dev_lifecycle.goal_handoff_active: Your AI team is already working on this goal.
dev_lifecycle.goal_handoff_confirm_q: Start your AI team on this goal?
dev_lifecycle.goal_handoff_stop_confirm_q: Stop the team working on this goal?
dev_lifecycle.goal_handoff_stop_explain: The team finishes whatever step it's mid-way through, then stops — it won't pick up new steps. The goal stays, so you can hand it back to the team later.
dev_lifecycle.goal_tasks_empty: No tasks yet — add one below, or hand the goal to your AI team.
dev_lifecycle.goal_map_drag_hint: Drag to arrange · zoom in for detail
dev_lifecycle.goal_map_tidy_hint: Re-arrange goals into status clusters (clears dragged positions)
dev_lifecycle.start_a_competition_desc: Spawn 2–4 competitors with different strategies to tackle the same task. The winner's approach gets promoted.
dev_lifecycle.cost_warning_desc: each competitor runs the full task independently — this uses {count}× more tokens.
dev_lifecycle.select_project_for_competitions: Select a project to see competitions.
dev_lifecycle.no_competitions_yet: No competitions yet. Start one to have 2–4 strategies race to solve a task.
dev_lifecycle.no_goals_yet_project: No goals yet. Create goals in the Project Manager to track progress.
dev_lifecycle.no_goals_kanban: No goals yet. Create goals to see them here.
dev_lifecycle.first_slot_bias: First slot biased toward previous winner's approach.
dev_lifecycle.leaderboard_subtitle: Aggregated across resolved competitions. Higher wins.
dev_lifecycle.capture_insight_desc: What made this approach win? This note is stored with the winner and used to seed future competitions.
dev_lifecycle.insight_placeholder: Describe what made this approach succeed…
dev_lifecycle.winning_genes_empty: No resolved competitions to analyze yet
dev_lifecycle.rematch_hint: Start a new competition with the winning strategy seeded in slot 1
dev_lifecycle.worktree_base_ref_help: Pick where each competitor's worktree branches from. Working tree continues your in-progress changes; Clean origin forks from the default branch on origin for a clean baseline.
dev_lifecycle.start_dev_server_title: Start dev server in this worktree
dev_lifecycle.dev_clone_desc: Dev Clone is an autonomous developer persona that manages your project lifecycle — competing strategies, healing errors, and shipping code.
dev_lifecycle.can_still_adopt: You can still adopt now — add GitHub URL and project folder later in project settings.
dev_lifecycle.spotlight_pick_goal: Pick a goal to inspect its dependencies and tasks.
dev_lifecycle.spotlight_tasks_empty: No tasks linked to this goal yet. Batch from accepted ideas in Task Runner.
dev_lifecycle.tracking_subtitle: Companion absorbs CLI activity per project — git commits and the active-runs ledger — and forms a stable picture every hour. Per-project toggles below; the master switch lives in Companion plugin setup.
dev_lifecycle.tracking_master_disabled_hint: Master tracking is currently OFF. Enable it in the Companion plugin setup to start hourly pulses.
dev_lifecycle.tracking_master_enabled_hint: Master tracking is ON. Per-project enable toggles below decide which projects feed the consolidator.
dev_lifecycle.tracking_no_projects: No projects registered. Use 'Register project' above to add the first one.
dev_lifecycle.tracking_obsidian_disabled_hint: Obsidian opt-in lands in Phase 6 once a vault credential is detected.
dev_lifecycle.tracking_obsidian_no_credential: Add an Obsidian credential in Connections to enable note watching.
dev_lifecycle.tracking_force_pulse_tooltip: Run the consolidator now — pulses all enabled projects, not just this one.
dev_lifecycle.tracking_pulse_fired: Pulse fired — refreshing in a few seconds.
dev_lifecycle.tracking_pulse_failed: Failed to fire pulse — check Companion plugin setup.
dev_lifecycle.winner_insight_saved: Winner selected. Insight saved to Dev Clone memory.
dev_lifecycle.winner_merge_when_ready: Winner selected. Merge the winning branch when ready.
dev_lifecycle.competition_cancelled_cleaning: Competition cancelled — cleaning up worktrees in background.
dev_lifecycle.readiness_ready: Ready to run. Dev Clone will scan, propose, and build on approval.
dev_lifecycle.readiness_partial: Partially configured. Click Auto-Setup to fill the gaps.
dev_lifecycle.readiness_not_configured: Not configured yet. Adopt Dev Clone and run Auto-Setup.

dev_projects.run_context_map_scan: Run Context Map scan for this project to populate metadata.
dev_projects.cross_project_map_subtitle: Aggregated from existing context maps. Click any project to expand.
dev_projects.shared_keywords_subtitle: Concepts present in multiple projects — may indicate shared architecture.
dev_projects.select_or_create: Select a project below or create a new one.
dev_projects.no_goals_add_below: No goals yet. Add one below.
dev_projects.standards_lint_hint: Block commits that fail the linter
dev_projects.standards_docs_hint: Require docs updated for user-visible changes
dev_projects.standards_automerge_hint: Use GitHub native auto-merge when checks pass
dev_projects.standards_scan_empty: No scan yet — run a golden-standard scan to check this repo.
dev_projects.create_codebase_connector_desc: Adds a "Codebase — {name}" connector so your agents can read this project without opening the credential catalog.
dev_projects.project_ready_desc: is ready. Would you like to generate a context map now?

whats_new.title: What's New
whats_new.subtitle_roadmap: What we're building now and what comes next.
whats_new.subtitle_changelog: What's new in your version of Personas.
whats_new.empty: Nothing to share for this release yet — check back soon.
whats_new.release_0_0_1_item_1_description: Design AI agents, run them on a schedule or trigger, and watch their work live on your desktop.
whats_new.release_0_0_2_summary: Connect Personas to other AI tools and read what's new without leaving the app.
whats_new.release_0_0_2_item_1_title: Open your agents to other AI tools
whats_new.release_0_0_2_item_1_description: Personas can now talk to other AI tools through a shared protocol. Pick exactly which agents you want to share, and protect them with access keys you control — your private agents stay private by default.
whats_new.release_0_0_2_item_2_title: Safer access for the desktop app
whats_new.release_0_0_2_item_2_description: The desktop app creates its own short-lived access key on every launch, so it can talk to its background services without exposing anything you didn't approve.
whats_new.release_0_0_2_item_3_description: See what's new in every Personas release without leaving the app. Switch between past versions, the current release, and the long-term roadmap from one place.
whats_new.release_0_0_2_item_4_title: Your agents learn from every session
whats_new.release_0_0_2_item_4_description: Personas now captures the lessons from each agent session, distills them into reusable knowledge, and brings them forward to the next run. Your agents get smarter the longer you use them — no manual training needed.
whats_new.release_0_0_2_item_5_title: Turn any document into a listenable briefing
whats_new.release_0_0_2_item_5_description: A new template that turns research reports, meeting transcripts, or long articles into a 5- to 15-minute two-host audio conversation you can listen to on the go. You pick the style — casual, formal, news, or executive — and review the script before the voices are generated.
whats_new.release_0_0_2_item_6_title: A Stanford-trained editor for your writing
whats_new.release_0_0_2_item_6_description: A new template that reviews your drafts using the Stanford "Writing in the Sciences" method. It flags passive voice, dead-weight phrases, and jargon — and explains exactly why every suggested change makes the sentence clearer. It learns your voice from the edits you accept or reject.
whats_new.release_0_0_2_item_7_title: Turn 20 hours of YouTube production into 3
whats_new.release_0_0_2_item_7_description: A new template compresses a full YouTube video cycle down to about three hours by handling everything except filming. It checks your niche is worth pursuing, drafts a hook-first script in your voice, and auto-removes retakes from raw footage using the approved script as ground truth — so you can film naturally, stumble freely, and keep going.
whats_new.release_0_0_2_item_8_title: Run Apify scrapers from your agents
whats_new.release_0_0_2_item_8_description: Connect any actor from the Apify platform — YouTube scrapers, Twitter scrapers, browser automation, and hundreds of others — directly to your agents. Unlocks research workflows that need data the official APIs cannot reach.
whats_new.release_0_0_2_item_9_description: Search recent tweets, track trending topics, and post directly from your agents. Useful for content research, social listening, and marketing automation without leaving Personas.
whats_new.release_0_0_2_item_10_description: The first video platform connector in Personas. Search videos, fetch channel statistics, pull trending content, and analyze competitors directly from any agent that needs to understand what is happening on YouTube.
whats_new.release_0_0_2_item_11_title: Turn audio into text with Deepgram
whats_new.release_0_0_2_item_11_description: A new speech-to-text connector that transcribes audio files or URLs with word-level timing. Powers the YouTube creator assistant for auto-editing, and gives any agent the ability to process voice notes, meeting recordings, or podcast content.
whats_new.release_0_0_2_item_12_title: Other AI tools can check on and stop your agents
whats_new.release_0_0_2_item_12_description: When another AI tool calls one of your shared agents, it can now ask 'is this still running?' and 'please stop now' in mid-flight. Long research jobs no longer have to keep the connection open the whole time, and stuck runs can be cancelled cleanly from the calling side. Each agent only ever sees its own runs, so nothing leaks across agents.
whats_new.release_0_0_2_item_13_description: Track traffic, run A/B experiments, and swap landing-page copy without touching your code. Personas can now pull heat maps and conversion data from Humbalytics and use them to guide your experiments.
whats_new.release_0_0_2_item_14_description: Agents can now read any website — even modern JavaScript-heavy ones — as clean, structured content. Useful for research, market intelligence, and content-monitoring agents.
whats_new.release_0_0_2_item_15_title: A marketing agent that actually runs your A/B tests
whats_new.release_0_0_2_item_15_description: A new template that proposes headline variants, deploys them with your approval, waits for statistical significance, and promotes winners automatically. Every change is human-gated before going live, and each experiment runs long enough for the results to actually mean something.
whats_new.release_0_0_2_item_16_title: Read your team's BI dashboards from any agent
whats_new.release_0_0_2_item_16_description: Personas can now pull results from saved Redash queries and dashboards. If your team already maintains shared dashboards, your agents read the same numbers humans see — no SQL rewriting, no CSV exports, no copy-paste.
whats_new.release_0_0_2_item_17_description: Your agents can run saved Metabase questions and read dashboards straight from your workspace. Works with Metabase Pro, Enterprise, and any self-hosted instance.
whats_new.release_0_0_2_item_18_title: Daily BI digest delivered to you
whats_new.release_0_0_2_item_18_description: A new template picks up the saved queries you already maintain in Redash or Metabase, summarizes the latest numbers each morning, and posts a plain-English digest with week-over-week anomaly flags. Your dashboards stay useful even when no one opens them.
whats_new.release_0_0_2_item_19_title: Replay lab runs as event streams
whats_new.release_0_0_2_item_19_description: The Lab now captures every assistant message, tool call, and result for each run. Open any past scenario and step through the timeline to see exactly how the agent reasoned and which tools it touched.
whats_new.release_0_0_2_item_20_description: Drive ingestion now extracts text from scanned PDFs and image-only documents using a local OCR backend with Claude as a fallback. Files stay on your machine; nothing is shipped to a cloud OCR service.
whats_new.release_0_0_2_item_21_description: Add a journal coach to your Obsidian vault that reads each daily entry, surfaces the past notes that ground it, and notices recurring themes before you do. Tone is yours to pick — gentle, direct, or socratic. The reflection writes back into the same daily note, so your journal stays the single source of truth.
whats_new.release_roadmap_summary: What we're building now and what comes next.
whats_new.release_roadmap_item_2_description: Run your agents 24/7 in the cloud and stream their work back to your desktop in real time.
whats_new.release_roadmap_item_3_description: A web companion for accounts, billing, and managing agents from any browser.
whats_new.release_roadmap_item_4_description: Full multi-language support, including right-to-left layouts and language-specific fonts.
whats_new.release_roadmap_item_6_description: Build agents together with your team — shared spaces, role permissions, and live dashboards.

kpis.title: KPIs
kpis.subtitle: Success metrics that steer the team — goals get derived when a KPI drifts off track
kpis.scan_failed: Could not start the KPI scan
kpis.empty_hint: Run a KPI scan to get grounded proposals from your codebase and context map.
kpis.empty_with_proposals_hint: Proposals are waiting in the review queue — accept the ones that matter.
kpis.connect_tooltip: This KPI needs the {service} connector to be measurable. Open the credential catalog to set it up.
kpis.queue_empty_hint: Run a KPI scan to fill this queue, or check back after the next scheduled scan.
kpis.archive_confirm_body: Archived KPIs stop being measured and are fed back to future scans as rejected examples. The measurement history is kept.
kpis.explainer: KPIs define what success means for this project. The scan proposes them from your code, measurements track them over time, and when one drifts off track the system derives a goal for your team to fix it — the next measurement shows whether it worked.
kpis.derived_qa_bounce: how often QA sends work back for fixes
kpis.derived_exec_failure: how often the team's runs fail
kpis.derived_parked_age: how long work waits for your sign-off
kpis.describe_derived: Tracked {cadence} from the team's own workflow — {what}.
kpis.describe_derived_generic: Tracked {cadence} from the team's own workflow.
kpis.describe_manual: You enter this value yourself whenever you have it.
kpis.pace_on_dated: On pace — {pct}% of the way to {target} {unit}, {days} days left.
kpis.pace_off: Falling behind — at {current} {unit} against a target of {target} {unit}.
kpis.pace_off_dated: Falling behind — {current} {unit} now, {target} {unit} needed in {days} days.
kpis.evidence_tooltip: This baseline was measured from your repository during the scan — not estimated.
kpis.linked_goals_hint: {count} goal(s) derived from this KPI — markers on the chart show where they landed.
kpis.chart_no_data: The story chart appears after the first measurement.
kpis.chart_legend: Dashed line = target ({target} {unit}). Vertical marks = derived goals.
kpis.wizard_type_hint: Pick the metric type. Sources are matched to the type, never to a single tool.
kpis.wizard_no_matches: Nothing in your vault can answer this metric type yet.
kpis.wizard_testing_note: Then it runs once against the live API to prove the number comes back.
kpis.wizard_request_section: The exact request it will replay
kpis.wizard_freeze_note_recipe: A pre-built, tested procedure. Every future measurement replays it exactly — no AI at runtime.
kpis.wizard_freeze_note_llm: Composed once for this wiring. After you confirm, it is frozen and replayed exactly — no AI at runtime.
kpis.source_none_hint: Recorded by hand. Connect a live source to measure automatically.
kpis.source_degraded_banner: The last replay against {service} failed. Automatic measurement is paused until you reconnect.
